title: Quiz: Ansatz Design Principles
description: Test your understanding of ansatz design for variational algorithms

questions.question: What is the primary tradeoff in ansatz design?
questions.explanation: The main tradeoff is between expressibility (ability to represent diverse states) and trainability (ease of optimization). Highly expressive ansätze may suffer from barren plateaus.

questions.question: What does 'expressibility' of an ansatz measure?

options.text: How well it can explore the state space

questions.explanation: Expressibility measures how well the ansatz can represent diverse quantum states. A highly expressive ansatz can reach states distributed uniformly in Hilbert space.

questions.question: Which entanglement pattern is most hardware-friendly for superconducting qubits?
questions.explanation: Linear (nearest-neighbor) connectivity matches the native topology of most superconducting quantum processors, avoiding the need for SWAP gates.

questions.question: Adding more layers to an ansatz always improves performance.
questions.explanation: False. While more layers increase expressibility, they also increase circuit depth (more noise), parameter count (harder optimization), and risk of barren plateaus.

questions.question: What is the UCCSD ansatz designed for?
questions.explanation: UCCSD (Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles) is designed for quantum chemistry applications, specifically finding molecular ground states while preserving symmetries.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of problem-specific ansätze over generic ones?

options.text: They leverage problem symmetries to reduce search space
They work on all problems equally well

questions.explanation: Problem-specific ansätze incorporate known symmetries (particle number, spin, etc.), reducing the effective parameter space and often improving trainability.

questions.question: What characterizes a 'hardware-efficient' ansatz?

options.text: Uses only quantum gates, no classical processing
Uses native gates and connectivity of the target hardware
Has the minimum possible number of parameters

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansätze are designed to use the native gate set and connectivity of the target quantum processor, minimizing the need for compilation overhead.

questions.question: Which factors affect circuit depth in an ansatz? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Number of layers directly sets depth. Entanglement patterns may require SWAP gates. Gate decomposition expands non-native gates. The classical optimizer doesn't affect circuit depth.
